phase_1.audio: Audio recording captures initial spike of 94.9 dBA, gradually settling to 64.9-73.9 dBA midway through session. One or more speech segments identified (avg confidence 0.66), suggesting social environment. Higher ambient noise detected; concentration requires active focus effort.
phase_1.vitals: Heart rate averaged 95 bpm throughout session (consistently elevated, suggesting heightened stress or engagement). Data indicates user was in suboptimal physiological state for concentration.
phase_1.gps_motion: GPS signal strength inconsistent at start (2.6m error margin), achieving 2.3m lock after stabilization. Kinetic data shows user establishing stable position within first seconds; minimal body movement thereafter.

phase_2.correlations: Positional settling (GPS convergence) corresponds to kinetic patterns. Acoustic fluctuation observed.
phase_2.holistic_assessment: Session shows moderate concentration quality with mixed environmental factors. Acoustic baseline of 73.9 dB provides challenging conditions. User maintained consistent positioning.

phase_3.recommendations: Modest noise control (e.g., ambient music, quiet background) could improve focus. Moderate session length adequate; potential to extend to 337s for skill building.

reason: Score determined by comprehensive assessment: ambient noise management (73.9dB), user-state stability (settled), physiological data (available), and sustainable duration (225s).
